mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, it signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending across durable and non-durable sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and tighter capacity utilization, which transmit to equity markets as positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant during late-cycle or recessionary phases, with a regime-switching model showing coefficient magnitude drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles, or the relationship flips negative during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, it signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the threshold effect disappears in a walk-forward test with a p-value above 0.05 for the dummy variable.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when payrolls rise but unemployment remains flat, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500 thousand units signal a sharp contraction in residential construction activity, which drags down related supply chains and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC < 0.02 during months where housing starts fall below 500k, or the threshold dummy loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling window.
